name: Mark Morrison
bio: Mark Morrison has a durable r&b / hip-hop soul catalog that continues to attract listeners through streaming, playlists, and replay value.
notable_for: Mark Morrison remains closely associated with Return of the Mack and Crazy, which still anchor attention around the catalog.
career_highlight: Songs like Return of the Mack and Crazy still help define the catalog's long-tail earnings profile.
revenue_drivers: Recognizable catalog cuts keep drawing repeat streaming long after the original release cycle.
Playlist memory and cultural recall help the strongest records stay active.
Sampling, sync use, and nostalgia spikes can lift the baseline.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps Mark Morrison's current headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: Mark Morrison's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
